AudioProjectConverter.Title: Audio Project Converter
AudioProjectConverter.Description: The Audio Project Converter is a tool for creating an Audio Project out of Dialogue Events in the provided .bnk files. It takes the VO Actor substring(s) and searches the Dialogue Events in the supplied .bnk files for their usage and converts that to the Audio Project format. Read the tooltips for more information.
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.AudioProj: The name of your converted Audio Project.
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.OutputDir: The pack directory where the converted Audio Project is output to. There needs to be a file in the directory for it to show.
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.VoActorSu: A substring common to all the VO Actor keys whose usage you want converted to the Audio Project format. You can include multiple substrings using a comma to separate them e.g. "ovn_vo_actor_Araby, ovn_vo_actor_Albion".
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.WemsDirec: A directory on your computer containing all the .wem files used in the soundbank (just use all wem files if you don't know which are used).
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.BnksDirec: A directory on your computer containing all the .bnk files to be searched.
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.Convertin: Tick this box if you need to get the States used in the soundbank from the Wwise project. There's no need to tick if you're converting a vanilla .bnk or the pack has a states_data.dat in the pack for the .bnk you're converting as that contains the States data for a or .bnk made by the Audio Editor.
AudioProjectConverter.Tooltips.Soundbank: The path to the SoundbanksInfo.xml should be "your_wwise_project_folder\GeneratedSoundBanks\Windows\SoundbanksInfo.xml". This contains the States data for the Wwise project.
AudioProjectMerger.Title: Audio Project Merger
AudioProjectMerger.Description: The Audio Project Merger is a tool for merging two Audio Projects.
AudioProjectMerger.Tooltips.MergedAudioP: The name of your merged Audio Project.
AudioProjectMerger.Tooltips.OutputDirect: The pack directory where the merged Audio Project is output to. There needs to be a file in the directory for it to show.
AudioProjectMerger.Tooltips.BaseAudioPro: The path to the Audio Project that you want to merge the Merging Audio Project into.
AudioProjectMerger.Tooltips.MergingAudio: The path to the Audio Project that you want to merge into the base Audio Project.
DialogueEventMerger.Title: Dialogue Event Merger
DialogueEventMerger.Description: The Dialogue Event Merger is a tool for merging Dialogue Events from modded SoundBanks with Dialogue Events from vanilla into a single SoundBank. Read the tooltips for more information.
DialogueEventMerger.Tooltips.SoundBankSu: The suffix of your merged SoundBanks.
DialogueEventMerger.Tooltips.ModdedSound: Select one or more modded SoundBanks from which their Dialogue Events will be merged with vanilla.

AudioProjectExplorer.Tooltips.DialogueEv: Dialogue Event Type refers to the type of action or trigger in game that the Dialogue Event relates to. Select the Dialogue Event type to filter by that.
The Dialogue Event profile refers to the Select the Dialogue Event profile to filter by that
AudioProjectExplorer.Tooltips.ShowEdited: Filters the Audio Project Explorer tree to show only items that have been edited.
AudioProjectExplorer.Tooltips.ShowAction: Filters the Audio Project Explorer tree to show or hide Action Events.
AudioProjectExplorer.Tooltips.ShowDialog: Filters the Audio Project Explorer Tree to show or hide Dialogue Events.
AudioEditor.Settings.Title: Settings
AudioEditor.Settings.ShowSettingsFromVie: Show Settings from Audio Project Viewer
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.SetRecomme: When multiple audio files are set, this sets the container's settings to what is recommended for a container containing VO audio files.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.RemoveAudi: Remove the selected Audio Files from the Settings.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.ShowSettin: When selecting items in the Audio Project Viewer with the selected the settings displayed will be that item's settings. When this is not selected the settings displayed will be the settings of the item in the Audio Project Editor.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.ContainerT: The Container Type determines the order in which objects are played.
Sequence - Plays objects in the sequence in which they were added.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.RandomType: The Container Type determines the order in which objects are played.
Shuffle - Plays objects at random without repeating an object until the entire pool of objects has been played.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.Repetition: Repitition Interval determines how many other objects must be played before an object can be repeated.
If Random Type is Standard, the object played is selected completely randomly, but the last n objects played are excluded from the list.
If Random Type is Shuffle, when the container is looped, the last n objects played will be excluded from the list.
If Random Type is Shuffle, non-looping and Play Mode continuous, the Repetition Interval has no effect on the playback of the container.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.PlaylistEn: Playlist End Behaviour determines how the container behaves after it has played the last object in the playlist.
Restart - Plays the playlist in the same order.
Play In Reverse Order - Plays the playlist in reverse order.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.PlayModeLi: The Play Mode determines whether a single object or the full playlist is played when triggered.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.PlayModeSt: Step - Plays one object in the playlist when triggered.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.PlayModeCo: Continuous - Plays the complete playlist when triggered.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.AlwaysRese: Always Reset Container determines whether playback continues or returns to the start of the playlist when a container is replayed after being stopped during playback.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.LoopingTyp: The Looping Type handles how many times a container is played.
Disabled - The container does not loop playback.
Finite Looping - The container playback loops according to the Number of Loops.
Infinite Looping - The container playback loops infinitely.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.NumberOfLo: The Number of Loops determines how many times a container is played if Looping Type is set to Finite Looping.
AudioEditor.Settings.Tooltips.Transition: The Transition Type handles the transition between objects in the container.
Xfade (amp) - Adds a crossfade between two objects using constant amplitude.
Xfade (power) - Adds a crossfade between two objects using constant power.
Delay - Adds a silence between two objects.
Sample accurate - Creates seamless transitions with no latency between objects.
Trigger rate - Determines the rate at which new objects within the container will be played. This option is useful for simulating rapid gun fire.

Transition Duration is the length of the crossfades or delay between clips. If Trigger rate is selected as the transition type, the duration defines the amount of time that will pass before the next sound is triggered.
The Duration slider is unavailable when the Sample Accurate option is selected.

The range is between 0 and 3600 seconds. The maximum duration of a crossfade is equal to half the duration of the sound. In cases where the crossfade time is longer than half the duration of the sound, the crossfade duration is automatically adjusted to the maximum allowed. This automatic adjustment is performed by the sound engine at run time. Wwise does not limit or indicate that the crossfade time is too long for some of the sounds in the container.
AudioFilesExplorer.Tooltips.SetAudioFile: Set the selected audio files as the audio files in the Settings.
AudioFilesExplorer.Tooltips.AddToAudioFi: Add the selected audio files to the audio files already set in the Settings.
AudioProjectViewer.Tooltips.EditRowLine1: Removes the selected row from the Audio Project and adds it to the Audio Project Editor.
AudioProjectViewer.Tooltips.RemoveRowLin: Removes the selected row(s) from the Audio Project.
AudioProjectViewer.Tooltips.DataGridLine: To edit or remove rows select them.
To copy and paste rows (Dialogue Events rows only) select them and right-click or use the shortcuts:
AudioProjectEditor.Tooltips.AddRowLine1: Adds the row to the Audio Project.
AudioProjectEditor.Tooltips.ShowModdedSt: Shows only modded states in the drop downs rather than modded states and vanilla states.
